title: Transmission Security — Unencrypted SMTP with PHI Patterns

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.312(e)(1) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.312(e)(1) (Transmission security) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.26: Transmission Security — Unencrypted SMTP with PHI Patterns.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.26' running on index=mail sourcetype=proofpoint:mta or ms:o365:reporting MessageTrace, stream:smtp metadata, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809)
spl:
index=mail sourcetype="proofpoint:mta" earliest=-24h
| eval tls_used=if(match(lower(smtp_tls_status),"none|no|plaintext"),0,1)
| regex subject="(?i)\b(MRN|medical record|DOB|date of birth)\b"
| where tls_used=0
| stats count values(rcpt) as recipients by sender, subject, client_ip
| sort - count
description: Alerts when internal mail relays accept messages on port 25 without TLS that contain medical record number or DOB patterns, evidencing safeguards for ePHI in motion.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes mail and sourcetypes proofpoint:mta, proofpoint:mta`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Prefer DLP classification fields when available instead of regex on body; (2) route to messaging team for forced TLS connectors; (3) document exceptions for legacy lab devices with compensating controls. Indexes required: `index=mail`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=proofpoint:mta`, `sourcetype=proofpoint:mta``.
visualization: Table (sender, subject snippet, recipients), Single value (plaintext PHI candidates).

grandmaExplanation: We watch for alerts when internal mail relays accept messages on port 25 without tls that contain medical record number or dob patterns, evidencing safeguards for ephi in motion. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=mail` `sourcetype=proofpoint:mta` or `ms:o365:reporting` MessageTrace, `stream:smtp` metadata.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Prefer DLP classification fields when available instead of regex on body; (2) route to messaging team for forced TLS connectors; (3) document exceptions for legacy lab devices with compensating controls.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=mail sourcetype="proofpoint:mta" earliest=-24h
| eval tls_used=if(match(lower(smtp_tls_status),"none|no|plaintext"),0,1)
| regex subject="(?i)\b(MRN|medical record|DOB|date of birth)\b"
| where tls_used=0
| stats count values(rcpt) as recipients by sender, subject, client_ip
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transmission Security — Unencrypted SMTP with PHI Patterns (§164.312(e)(1))** — Alerts when internal mail relays accept messages on port 25 without TLS that contain medical record number or DOB patterns, evidencing safeguards for ePHI in motion.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=mail` `sourcetype=proofpoint:mta` or `ms:o365:reporting` MessageTrace, `stream:smtp` metadata. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: mail; **sourcetype**: proofpoint:mta. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=mail, sourcetype="proofpoint:mta", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **tls_used** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters rows matching a pattern with `regex`.
• Filters the current rows with `where tls_used=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sender, subject, client_ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sender, subject snippet, recipients), Single value (plaintext PHI candidates).
